name: Manganeses de la Polvorosa to Bod
name_original: Manganeses de la Polvorosa a Bodegas de Zorrilla — circular desde Villabrázaro
description: This moderate 18.2-mile mountain biking route from Villabrázaro offers countryside views and takes about 2 hours and 19 minutes.
region_intro: Mountain biking around Villabrázaro, located in the province of Zamora, Castilla y León, Spain, offers routes primarily through an agricultural environment. The landscape features pastures, cornfields, dryland cereals, and oak trees, with an average elevation of 724 meters. The region is characterized by relatively flat or gently rolling terrain, including sections of the historic Vía de la Plata and river valleys. While Villabrázaro itself provides gentle cycling opportunities, the broader Zamora province, particularly the Sierra de la Culebra, offers more demanding mountain biking routes. Best mountain bike trails around Villabrázaro The most popular mountain bike trail is Santa Cristina de la Polvorosa a Peña Redonda — circular desde Benavente, a difficult 35.0 miles (56.4 km) trail that takes 5 hours 0 minutes to complete. This route offers significant elevation gain through varied terrain. Another top favourite among local mountain bikers is Vía de la Plata Greenway and Iron Bridge Loop, an easy 13.6 miles (21.9 km) path. This route follows the Vía de la Plata, known for its gentle slopes and compacted earth. Local mountain bikers also love the Benavente and La Polvorosa Valley Loop, a 29.5 miles (47.5 km) trail leading through open fields and river valleys, often completed in about 3 hours 33 minutes. Mountain biking around Villabrázaro is defined by agricultural fields, river valleys, and sections of the Vía de la Plata. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy greenways to more challenging routes. The routes in Villabrázaro are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.4 stars from more than 10 reviews. More than 200 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore Villabrázaro's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many mountain bike trails are available around Villabrázaro?
A: There are over 30 mountain bike trails around Villabrázaro, offering a variety of routes for different preferences and skill levels. These routes have been explored by more than 200 mountain bikers using komoot.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on mountain bike trails near Villabrázaro?
A: The terrain around Villabrázaro is primarily agricultural, characterized by pastures, cornfields, dryland cereals, and oak trees. You'll find relatively flat or gently rolling paths, including sections of the historic Vía de la Plata and routes through river valleys. For more challenging, mountainous terrain, the Sierra de la Culebra is a significant option within the broader Zamora province, though it requires a separate trip.

Q: Are there mountain bike trails for different skill levels in the Villabrázaro area?
A: Yes, the area offers trails for various skill levels. You can find 4 easy routes, 24 moderate routes, and 3 difficult routes. This includes gentle greenways suitable for casual rides and more demanding trails with significant elevation gain.

Q: Are there any easy mountain bike trails suitable for beginners or families?
A: Yes, there are easy trails perfect for beginners or families. A great option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1232563315/via-de-la-plata-greenway-and-iron-bridge-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Vía de la Plata Greenway and Iron Bridge Loop&lt;/a&gt;, an easy 21.9 km path known for its gentle slopes and compacted earth, making it very accessible.

Q: What do other mountain bikers enjoy most about the trails in Villabrázaro?
A: The mountain bike trails around Villabrázaro are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.4 stars from over 10 reviews. Riders often praise the tranquil agricultural landscapes, the variety of routes from easy greenways to more challenging paths, and the opportunity to explore river valleys and historical routes like the Vía de la Plata.

Q: Are there any circular mountain bike routes available?
A: Yes, many routes are circular. For example, the challenging &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1230876731/santa-cristina-de-la-polvorosa-a-pena-redonda-circular-desde-benavente" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Santa Cristina de la Polvorosa a Peña Redonda — circular desde Benavente&lt;/a&gt; is a 56.4 km loop with significant elevation gain. Another popular option is the moderate &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1232421845/benavente-and-la-polvorosa-valley-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Benavente and La Polvorosa Valley Loop&lt;/a&gt;, which covers 47.5 km through open fields and river valleys.

Q: What are some notable landmarks or points of interest I can see along the mountain bike trails?
A: While riding, you might encounter several interesting sights. Keep an eye out for historical bridges such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4092040" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puente de la Vizana&lt;/a&gt;, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2810181" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Bridge over the Esla River&lt;/a&gt;, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5422594" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Villanueva de Azoague Iron Bridge&lt;/a&gt;. The town of &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2720898" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Benavente&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5151970" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Church of Santa María del Azogue&lt;/a&gt; are also nearby attractions.

Q: Is it possible to find more challenging mountain biking routes near Villabrázaro?
A: For more demanding mountain biking with significant elevation changes and technical trails, you would need to venture to the Sierra de la Culebra within the Zamora province. This mountain range offers moderate-to-high difficulty routes and peaks exceeding 1000 meters, known for its rolling relief and wooded slopes. Villabrázaro itself offers more gentle cycling experiences.

Q: Are there any trails that follow the Vía de la Plata?
A: Yes, several routes incorporate sections of the historic Vía de la Plata. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1232563315/via-de-la-plata-greenway-and-iron-bridge-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Vía de la Plata Greenway and Iron Bridge Loop&lt;/a&gt; is an easy 21.9 km trail that follows this path, characterized by its gentle slopes. Another option is the moderate &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1232546515/via-verde-de-la-plata-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Vía Verde de la Plata Trail&lt;/a&gt;, a 34.8 km route.

Q: What is the best time of year to go mountain biking in Villabrázaro?
A: The region's climate generally allows for pleasant cycling during spring and autumn, when temperatures are milder. Summer can be warm, and winter might bring cooler, potentially wetter conditions. Always check local weather forecasts before heading out.

Q: Are there any long-distance mountain bike routes in the area?
A: Yes, there are several longer routes for those looking for extended rides. The difficult &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1230876731/santa-cristina-de-la-polvorosa-a-pena-redonda-circular-desde-benavente" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Santa Cristina de la Polvorosa a Peña Redonda — circular desde Benavente&lt;/a&gt; is a substantial 56.4 km trail, while the moderate &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1232421845/benavente-and-la-polvorosa-valley-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Benavente and La Polvorosa Valley Loop&lt;/a&gt; covers 47.5 km.
tips: It's not the only singletrack near Morales de Rey but I still like it. He is not overly technical and has appeared in several races around here.
Near the town of Morales de Rey is the top of Peña Redonda, located at 815 meters above sea level with wonderful panoramic views.

Here you will find traditional cellars dug into the slopes of El Teso and Peña Redonda. These wineries, historically were a meeting place for neighbors and wine production. The largest number of this type of winery in the region is located here near Morales del Rey.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to complete this mountain biking route?
faqs.answer: The route is approximately 29.2 kilometers (18.1 miles) long. Komoot estimates the average duration to be around 2 hours and 19 minutes for mountain biking, but this can vary based on your fitness level and how often you stop.

faqs.question: What is the difficulty level and terrain like on this trail?
faqs.answer: This route is graded as moderate. While specific details for this exact trail are not widely published, routes in the Zamora region often feature a mix of dirt roads, gravel paths, and potentially some unpaved segments. Given its moderate rating, expect some sections that may require good fitness or could be challenging to ride.

faqs.question: Where does the trail start, and is there parking available?
faqs.answer: This circular route starts and ends near Villabrázaro. While specific parking areas are not detailed in public records for this exact route, you would typically look for parking within or on the outskirts of Villabrázaro. Consulting local maps or cycling apps for user-generated routes might provide more precise parking suggestions.

faqs.question: What points of interest or landmarks can I expect to see along the way?
faqs.answer: Along the route, you can experience the Singletrack of Morales de Rey, enjoy a View of Peña Redonda, and pass by Bodegas de Zorrilla. The general area offers pleasant countryside scenery typical of Zamora.

faqs.question: Is this mountain biking route dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: There are no specific restrictions mentioned regarding dogs on this route. Generally, dogs are welcome on trails in rural Spain, but it's always best to keep them under control, especially when passing through agricultural areas or near livestock. Please ensure you clean up after your pet.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to mountain bike this trail?
faqs.answer: The best time to ride this trail is typically during spring (April-May) and autumn (September-October) when temperatures are milder and the weather is generally more stable. Summers can be very hot in this region of Spain, and winters can bring colder temperatures and potentially wet conditions.

faqs.question: Does this trail intersect with any other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, sections of this route briefly overlap with other paths. You will find it intersects with parts of the Mtb Vía de la Plata, the Vía de la Plata hiking - 03 Salamanca to Astorga, Calle Corona, Calle Silos, and Lugar Diseminados.

faqs.question: Are there any permits or entrance fees required to use this trail?
faqs.answer: No, there are no known permits or entrance fees required to access this mountain biking route. It appears to be a publicly accessible trail.

faqs.question: What should I bring for a mountain bike ride on this route?
faqs.answer: Given the moderate difficulty and length, you should bring plenty of water, snacks, a repair kit for your bike, a helmet, appropriate cycling attire, and sun protection. A fully charged phone with the Komoot app or a GPS device is also highly recommended for navigation.

faqs.question: Is this route suitable for beginners or families?
faqs.answer: Rated as moderate, this route might be challenging for absolute beginners or very young children. It requires good fitness and some experience with mountain biking, especially if there are unpaved or technical sections. Families with experienced riders might enjoy it, but it's not designed as an easy, beginner-friendly path.

faqs.question: Is there any specific local advice for riding this trail?
faqs.answer: Since specific details for this named trail are not widely available in public records, it's highly recommended to consult local maps or cycling apps that might have user-generated routes. These resources can provide the most up-to-date and precise details on current terrain conditions and any informal track variations.
